content:
# Joshua Chapter 6 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 229-230 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_006_page_001.jpg`

---

**6:1** And-Jericho [was] shutting and-shut-up from-before [the]-sons of-Israel; none going-out and-none coming-in.

**6:2** And-said YHWH to Joshua, "See, I-have-given into-your-hand Jericho and-its-king, mighty-men of-valor.

**6:3** And-you-shall-go-around the-city, all [the]-men of-war, going-around the-city one time. Thus you-shall-do six days.

**6:4** And-seven priests shall-carry seven trumpets of-rams'-horns before the-ark. And-on-the-day the-seventh you-shall-go-around the-city seven times, and-the-priests shall-blow on-the-trumpets.

**6:5** And-it-shall-be, at-[the]-drawing-out of-[the]-horn of-the-ram, when-you-hear the-sound of-the-trumpet, all the-people shall-shout [with] a-great shout, and-the-wall of-the-city shall-fall beneath-it, and-the-people shall-go-up, each-man straight-before-him."

**6:6** And-called Joshua son of-Nun to the-priests, and-said to-them, "Carry the-ark of-the-covenant, and-seven priests shall-carry seven trumpets of-rams'-horns before [the]-ark of-YHWH."

**6:7** And-he-said to the-people, "Pass-over and-go-around the-city, and-the-armed-one shall-pass-over before [the]-ark of-YHWH."

**6:8** And-it-was, when-Joshua-spoke to the-people, and-the-seven priests carrying seven trumpets of-rams'-horns before YHWH passed-over and-blew on-the-trumpets, and-[the]-ark of-[the]-covenant of-YHWH [was] going after-them.

**6:9** And-the-armed-ones [were] going before the-priests, the-blowers of-the-trumpets, and-the-rear-guard [was] going after the-ark, going and-blowing on-the-trumpets.

**6:10** And-the-people, Joshua commanded, saying, "You-shall-not shout, and-you-shall-not cause-to-be-heard your-voice, and-a-word shall-not go-out from-your-mouth, until the-day of-my-saying to-you, 'Shout!' And-you-shall-shout."

**6:11** And-he-caused-to-go-around [the]-ark of-YHWH the-city, going-around one time. And-they-came to the-camp, and-they-lodged in-the-camp.

**6:12** And-rose-early Joshua in-the-morning, and-the-priests carried [the]-ark of-YHWH.

**6:13** And-seven the-priests carrying seven trumpets of-the-rams'-horns before [the]-ark of-YHWH [were] going, going and-blowing on-the-trumpets, and-the-armed-ones [were] going before-them, and-the-rear-guard [was] going after [the]-ark of-YHWH, going and-blowing on-the-trumpets.

**6:14** And-they-went-around the-city on-the-day the-second one time, and-they-returned to-the-camp. Thus they-did six days.

**6:15** And-it-was on-the-day the-seventh, and-they-rose-early as-[the]-going-up of-the-dawn, and-they-went-around the-city according-to-this manner seven times; only on-that day they-went-around the-city seven times.

**6:16** And-it-was at-the-time the-seventh, the-priests blew on-the-trumpets, and-said Joshua to the-people, "Shout! For YHWH has-given to-you the-city.

**6:17** And-the-city shall-be devoted-[to-destruction], she and-all which [is] in-her, to-YHWH. Only Rahab the-harlot shall-live, she and-all who [are] with-her in-the-house, for she hid the-messengers whom we-sent.

**6:18** And-surely you, keep [yourselves] from the-devoted-thing, lest you-devote [yourselves] and-you-take from the-devoted-thing, and-you-set the-camp of-Israel for-a-devoted-thing, and-you-trouble it.

**6:19** And-all silver and-gold, and-vessels of-bronze and-iron, [are] holy to-YHWH; into [the]-treasury of-YHWH they-shall-come."

**6:20** And-the-people shouted, and-they-blew on-the-trumpets. And-it-was when-the-people-heard the-sound of-the-trumpet, and-the-people shouted [with] a-great shout, and-the-wall fell beneath-it, and-the-people went-up into-the-city, each-man straight-before-him, and-they-captured the-city.

**6:21** And-they-devoted-[to-destruction] all which [was] in-the-city, from-man and-unto woman, from-young and-unto old, and-unto ox, and-sheep, and-donkey, with-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword.

**6:22** And-to-the-two men, the-ones-spying-out the-land, Joshua said, "Go into [the]-house of-the-woman, the-harlot, and-bring-out from-there the-woman and-all which [belongs]-to-her, as-you-swore to-her."

**6:23** And-went-in the-young-men, the-spies, and-they-brought-out Rahab and-her-father and-her-mother and-her-brothers and-all which [belonged]-to-her, and all her-clans they-brought-out. And-they-set-them from-outside to-[the]-camp of-Israel.

**6:24** And-the-city they-burned with-fire, and-all which [was] in-her. Only the-silver and-the-gold, and-[the]-vessels of-the-bronze and-the-iron, they-gave [into the]-treasury of-[the]-house of-YHWH.

**6:25** And-Rahab the-harlot, and-[the]-house of-her-father, and-all which [belonged]-to-her, Joshua let-live. And-she-dwelt in-[the]-midst of-Israel unto this day, for she hid the-messengers whom Joshua sent to-spy-out Jericho.

**6:26** And-Joshua adjured [them] at-that time, saying, "Cursed [be] the-man before YHWH who shall-rise-up and-build this city, Jericho: with-his-firstborn he-shall-lay-its-foundation, and-with-his-youngest he-shall-set-up its-doors."

**6:27** And-YHWH was with Joshua, and-his-fame was in-all the-land.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Source
This translation was produced by direct reading of the Leningrad Codex (Codex Leningradensis, B19A, dated 1008/1009 CE), pages 229-230, as photographed by the West Semitic Research Project (Bruce E. Zuckerman). The text is written in the standard three-column format with full Tiberian vocalization and cantillation marks (te'amim). Cross-reference was made against the Codex Vaticanus (LXX, Vat.gr.1209, folio 166) for structural comparison.

### Translation Decisions and Notes

1. **6:1 -- sogereth u-mesuggereth (shutting and-shut-up):** A striking double expression using the Qal active participle (sogereth) and the Pual passive participle (mesuggereth) of the same root s-g-r. This conveys that Jericho was both actively shutting [its gates] and passively sealed/barricaded. The doubling is preserved literally. The LXX renders this with a single verb (synkekleismene, "shut up"), losing the Hebrew doubling.

2. **6:2 -- nathatti (I-have-given):** Qal perfect 1cs, prophetic perfect -- YHWH speaks of a future act as already completed. This is a characteristic Hebrew rhetorical device expressing divine certainty. Rendered "I-have-given" to preserve the perfective aspect.

3. **6:4 -- yovlei ha-yovelim (trumpets of-rams'-horns):** The word yovel refers to a ram or ram's horn, distinct from shofar (which appears in v.5 as qeren ha-yovel). The shofrot of yovelim are specifically ram's-horn trumpets, not metal trumpets (chatsotsrot). The term yovel is also the origin of "jubilee" (Leviticus 25:10).

4. **6:5 -- bi-mshokh be-qeren ha-yovel (at-[the]-drawing-out of-[the]-horn of-the-ram):** The infinitive construct of m-sh-kh (to draw, extend) refers to the prolonged blast. This is the teki'ah gedolah -- a long, sustained blast, not a short staccato. The preposition bet with the infinitive marks temporal simultaneity.

5. **6:5 -- tachteyha (beneath-it):** Literally "under-her" (feminine singular suffix referring to the wall, which is feminine in Hebrew). This detail specifies that the wall collapsed downward in its place (falling flat), not toppling outward. Archaeological evidence at Tell es-Sultan shows walls that fell straight down and outward, consistent with this description.

6. **6:9 -- ha-mechallets (the-armed-one):** Qal participle from ch-l-ts, "to strip off, arm for battle." This refers to the armed vanguard. In v.9, the term is singular (collective), while the context makes clear it refers to the armed contingent as a body.

7. **6:13 -- halokh ve-taqo'a (going and-blowing):** The infinitive absolute halokh paired with the finite verb taqo'a produces a continuous-action construction -- "going and blowing" simultaneously and persistently. This Hebrew construction (infinitive absolute + ve + finite verb) describes ongoing simultaneous action. It is preserved literally.

8. **6:17 -- cherem (devoted-[to-destruction]):** The root ch-r-m denotes something irrevocably devoted to YHWH, either for total destruction or for sanctuary use. The cherem ban meant complete annihilation -- nothing could be kept for personal use. Rendered "devoted-[to-destruction]" with the implied meaning bracketed, as the Hebrew carries both the sacral and destructive senses simultaneously.

9. **6:18 -- ve-raq 'attem shimru min-ha-cherem pen-tacharimmu (And-surely you, keep from the-devoted-thing, lest you-devote):** Note the wordplay on ch-r-m: the devoted things (cherem) can cause the taker to become cherem himself. The verb tacharimmu (Hiphil) means "you cause [yourselves/Israel] to be devoted to destruction."

10. **6:20 -- va-yippol ha-chomah tachteyha (and-the-wall fell beneath-it):** Same expression as v.5. The wall collapsed in situ, straight down. The singular "wall" (chomah) refers to the city's defensive perimeter as a unified structure.

11. **6:21 -- le-fi-cherev (with-[the]-mouth of-[the]-sword):** Literally "to [the] mouth of [the] sword." A standard Hebrew idiom for total slaughter by the sword. The "mouth" (pi) metaphorically pictures the sword as devouring.

12. **6:25 -- va-teshev be-qerev Yisra'el 'ad ha-yom ha-zeh (and-she-dwelt in-[the]-midst of-Israel unto this day):** The phrase "unto this day" is a narrative marker indicating the author wrote at a time when Rahab's descendants were still recognizable within Israel. This is significant for dating the composition of Joshua. The LXX Vaticanus preserves the same temporal notice.

13. **6:26 -- be-khoro yeyassedennah u-vi-tse'iro yatsiv delatheyha (with-his-firstborn he-shall-lay-its-foundation, and-with-his-youngest he-shall-set-up its-doors):** The preposition bet here means "at the cost of" -- rebuilding Jericho will cost the builder his firstborn and youngest sons. This curse was fulfilled in 1 Kings 16:34, when Hiel the Bethelite rebuilt Jericho and lost Abiram (his firstborn) and Segub (his youngest).

14. **6:15 -- ka-mishpat ha-zeh (according-to-this manner):** The word mishpat here means "manner, custom, prescribed way" rather than its more common sense of "judgment/justice." The seventh day's ritual followed the same pattern as the previous six, but repeated seven times.

15. **LXX Comparison (Vaticanus, folio 166):** The Vaticanus Greek text of Joshua 6 broadly corresponds to the MT but shows several expansions and minor rearrangements. Notably, the LXX adds explicit mention of the ark being carried in certain verses where the MT is more concise, and the order of some clauses in the marching instructions differs slightly. The cherem vocabulary is rendered with anathema in the Greek. These variants do not affect the core narrative but reflect different editorial traditions.

### Paragraph Structure (Petuchah/Setumah)
The Leningrad Codex marks a setumah (closed paragraph, indicated by samekh) after verse 27, marking the conclusion of the Jericho narrative before the Achan episode in chapter 7. Internal paragraph breaks appear within the chapter corresponding to shifts between YHWH's instructions (vv.2-5), Joshua's commands (vv.6-7, 10, 16-19), and narrative action (vv.8-9, 11-15, 20-27).
